definition.meaning: Oar, steering oar; paddle used for steering a vessel
definition.origin: From Proto-West Germanic *rōþr, from Proto-Germanic *rōþrą "oar, rudder"
definition.etymology: Related to Old English rōwan "to row"

definition.meaning: Broad flat piece of wood attached to the stern of a boat for steering (sense from c. 1300)
Oar, rudder, instrument for rowing or steering
definition.cognates: Old Frisian rōther, Old High German ruodar, Old Norse rōthr, Dutch roer, German Ruder

definition.meaning: To row, to move (root of rudder, row)
rōthr: oar/rudder; stjórnborði: steerboard (starboard side where rudder was mounted)
Rudder, helm; steering device; direction, guidance
definition.origin: Borrowed from Old Norse stýri "rudder, helm"
definition.note: Also figurative: any means of governing or directing a course

definition.origin: From Proto-Germanic *steuro "rudder, steering", extended from PIE *sta- "to stand, make firm"

definition.meaning: Any means of governing, directing, or guiding a course; a leader or principle
